name: Montaña Palentina Reservoirs Loop
name_original: Guardo desde Cervera de Pisuerga — circular por la Ruta de los Pantanos
description: This difficult 60.7-mile touring cycling route from Cervera de Pisuerga explores the scenic Ruta de los Pantanos in Montaña Palentina.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cervera de Pisuerga are set within the Palencia Mountains, part of the Cantabrian Mountains, offering a diverse landscape for outdoor activities. The region features mountainous terrain, dense forests, and expansive meadows, providing varied backdrops for cycling. Cyclists can explore river valleys and the scenic "Ruta de los Pantanos," which includes the Ruesga, Requejada, and Aguilar reservoirs. This area, nestled in the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, presents a mix of natural beauty and challenging routes. Best touring cycling routes around Cervera De Pisuerga The most popular touring cycling route is Montaña Palentina Reservoirs Loop, a 60.7 miles (97.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the scenic Ruta de los Pantanos. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Piedrasluengas Pass from Cervera de Pisuerga, a difficult 32.9 miles (53.0 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of the Picos de Europa mountains and the Cantabrian valleys. Local touring cyclists also love the Return to the Ruesga Reservoir from Cervera de Pisuerga, a 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the Ruesga Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Cervera De Pisuerga is defined by mountainous terrain, scenic reservoirs, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Cervera De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cervera De Pisuerga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes can I find around Cervera de Pisuerga?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Cervera de Pisuerga, offering a variety of experiences within the Palencia Mountains. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cervera de Pisuerga?
A: The region is characterized by its stunning mountainous terrain, dense forests, and expansive meadows, forming part of the Cantabrian Mountains. You'll encounter panoramic views, river valleys, and the scenic 'Ruta de los Pantanos' which explores several reservoirs. Routes range from moderate to challenging, often featuring significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cervera de Pisuerga?
A: While many routes in the area are challenging, there are options for less experienced cyclists. For example, the 'Senda del Oso' (Bear's Path), an easy 11 km circular route starting at San Roque bridge, offers a pleasant ride passing by the Requejada reservoir. The region also features two routes classified as easy within the komoot guide.

Q: Which routes offer the best views of the Picos de Europa mountains?
A: For breathtaking vistas of the Picos de Europa mountains and the Cantabrian valleys, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324986608/piedrasluengas-pass-from-cervera-de-pisuerga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Pass from Cervera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt;. This challenging 53.0 km route provides unique panoramic views from the Piedrasluengas Pass.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Cervera de Pisuerga?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323870576/montana-palentina-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montaña Palentina Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 97.7 km route that explores the scenic Ruta de los Pantanos. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170958335/requejada-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requejada Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 32.4 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural features can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Beyond the stunning mountain scenery, you can explore the scenic 'Ruta de los Pantanos' which includes the Ruesga, Requejada, and Aguilar reservoirs. The region also boasts the Rivet of Estalaya, home to the largest oak tree in the mountains, and the ancient Yew-tree forest of Tejada de Tosande. The area is rich in diverse flora and fauna as part of the Montaña Palentina Natural Park.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: Along your rides, you might encounter various points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular vistas. For those interested in local history and culture, Cervera de Pisuerga itself features the 16th-century Church of Santa María del Castillo and the unique early medieval Hermitage of San Vicente cave. You can also find several huts and shelters, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3394566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Golobar Refuge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5837344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Free shelter in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Aguilar Reservoir?
A: Yes, you can cycle to and around the Aguilar Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323909401/return-to-the-aguilar-reservoir-from-cervera-de-pisuerga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Aguilar Reservoir from Cervera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is a 51.4 km route that takes you to this scenic body of water. The 'Ruta de los Pantanos' also encompasses this reservoir.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cervera de Pisuerga?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cervera de Pisuerga are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscape, the challenging yet rewarding climbs, and the stunning natural beauty of the Montaña Palentina Natural Park.

Q: Is it possible to find accommodation or cafes near the cycling routes?
A: Cervera de Pisuerga serves as a central hub and offers various amenities, including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options suitable for cyclists. While specific cafes directly on every route are not listed, the town itself provides places to refuel and rest. You can also find several huts and shelters in the surrounding area, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5144390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue de Villaverde&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cervera de Pisuerga?
A: The region's mountainous climate means that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow to higher passes like Piedrasluengas, making some routes more challenging or inaccessible.
tips: Cervera de Pisuerga is a small tourist mountain town in the heart of the Palencia Mountain, part of the Cantabrian Mountains. The landscape and views will blow you away. An amazing place to bike and explore.

From this point you can enjoy authentic views of the Cabañal rock from the paved road itself.

Peña Cabañal is situated nearby to Alto de la Varga, and close to Loma Redonda.

Excellent viewpoint with an impressive panoramic view of the Camporredondo reservoir and its surroundings. Located in a privileged natural environment, the viewpoint allows you to enjoy the beauty of the mountainous landscape and the crystalline reservoir.

The Camporredondo reservoir regulates the course of the Carrión river and creates an irrigable area of great importance for the province. Built in 1930, the reservoir is part of the "Ruta de los Pantanos" together with the reservoirs of Aguilar de Campoo, Ruesga, Requejada and Compuerto.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires very good fitness. It spans nearly 98 kilometers with significant elevation changes (around 1,120 meters up and down). While mostly paved, there are unpaved sections that can be challenging, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ruta de los Pantanos cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly paved, with 88% to 91% consisting of asphalt or paved roads. However, expect some unpaved or gravel sections, making up 2% to 12% of the route. These unpaved segments can be difficult to ride, especially if you're carrying luggage or using narrower road tires.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is estimated to take approximately 6 hours to complete, assuming a steady cycling pace. This duration does not account for stops to enjoy the scenery or breaks.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience breathtaking scenery within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. Key features include the tranquil Camporredondo Reservoir, along with Compuerto, Ruesga, and Requejada reservoirs. Look out for imposing limestone peaks like Curavacas and Espigüete. Designated viewpoints, such as View of Camporredondo Reservoir from Mirador de Alba de los Cardaños and Alto de la Varga, offer spectacular panoramic vistas. You'll also pass through majestic oak and beech groves.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Ruta de los Pantanos?
faqs.answer: The route is particularly vibrant in autumn when the oak and beech groves display rich colors. Spring and early summer also offer pleasant conditions with lush landscapes. Always check the weather forecast before you go, as mountain conditions can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Cervera de Pisuerga?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Cervera de Pisuerga. You should be able to find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de los Pantanos cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily a cycling path, dogs are generally allowed in the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. However, given the length and difficulty of this specific cycling route, it might not be ideal for all dogs, especially for long stretches. If you bring your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, on a leash, and that you carry enough water for them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route or the Montaña Palentina Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas and wildlife.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the Camino Olvidado (Matamorisca-Puente Almuhey), Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, and Sendero Histórico: Palencia. You'll also briefly cross sections of the Asturian Way and [CIMA PA03] La Varga.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, it's essential to pack adequately. Bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate cycling gear for varying weather conditions. Sunscreen, a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone are also highly recommended. Consider carrying a map or GPS device, as phone signal can be unreliable in some mountain areas.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the Ruta de los Pantanos?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Cervera de Pisuerga, where you'll find various restaurants and cafes. Along the route, especially in smaller villages, options might be limited, so it's wise to carry sufficient provisions. Some villages near the reservoirs may have small shops or bars, but don't rely on them being open, especially outside peak season.
